zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Aldrich, Bailiff of Yarmouth於1460年6月1日出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可亞爾姆斯時，他的父親William Aldrich39歲，母親Mary Tudor39歲。他於1472年與Lady Alice Yarmouth在英國英格蘭諾福爾可格亞特 亞爾姆斯結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1518年4月14日在他的家鄉享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Aldrich, Bailiff of Yarmouth于1460年6月1日出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可亞爾姆斯时，他的父亲William Aldrich39岁，母亲Mary Tudor39岁。他于1472年与Lady Alice Yarmouth在英国英格兰諾福爾可格亞特 亞爾姆斯结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1518年4月14日在他的家乡享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: Aldrich,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Aldridge 1, from the Middle English personal names Alrich or Elrich and Aldrich or Eldrich.
History: The earliest recorded bearer of this surname in North America is George Alrich, who came from Derbyshire to MA in 1631.

variants.name: Arledge
meanings: English: variant of   Aldrich   or   Aldridge  . A few bearers of the name are recorded in southern England in the 17th–19th centuries, but the name appears to have died out in Britain.
History: Isaac Arledge died in Fairfield County, SC, in 1790.

variants.name: Aldridge
meanings: English:

from the Middle English personal names Alrich or Elrich and Aldrich or Eldrich, representing any one of three Old English personal names, Æthelrīc, Ælfrīc, and Ealdrīc, formed respectively from æthel- ‘noble’, ælf ‘elf, sprite’, and (e)ald ‘old, honored’ + rīc ‘ruler’.

habitational name from Aldridge (Staffordshire), Aldridge Grove in Hampden (Buckinghamshire), or from a similarly named unidentified place in the West Midlands, recorded in Domesday Book as Alrewic, from Old English alor ‘alder’ + wīc ‘specialized farmstead’.

nameMeanings.name: Bailiff
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for an officer of a court of justice, from Middle English bailli, baillif ‘manager, administrator’ (Old French bailli(s), baillif). The term could denote the king's officer in a county, hundred, town, castle, or forest, or an elected town officer, a magistrate, an officer of a court, serving warrants and enforcing judgments. In Middle English, a bailiff often denoted the agent of a lord in managing a manor, collecting rents, etc., but in the early modern period the term gradually because specialized as denoting an officer of justice under a sheriff, who executes writs and processes, distraints, and arrests. Compare the variant Bayliff , Bayliss and also Bailey .

variants.name: Bayliff
meanings: English: variant of   Bailiff  . See also   Bayliss  .

variants.name: Bayliss
meanings: English: variant of Bailiff , with excrescent -s, an occupational name for an officer of a court of justice, whose duties included serving writs, distraining goods, and (formerly) arresting people. In England it was formerly a status name for the chief officer of a hundred (administrative subdivision of a county). The derivation is from Middle English, Old French bailis, from Late Latin baiulivus (adjective), ‘pertaining to an attendant or porter’ (see Bailey ).
History: Thomas Baylies, a prominent Quaker, came to Boston from London in 1737.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Yarmouth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Great Yarmouth (Norfolk), Little Yarmouth (Suffolk), or Yarmouth (IoW).

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Aldrich, Bailiff of Yarmouth was born on 1 June 1460, in Yarmouth, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Aldrich, was 39 and his mother, Mary Tudor, was 39. He married Lady Alice Yarmouth in 1472, in Great Yarmouth, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 14 April 1518, in his hometown, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Aldrich, Bailiff of Yarmouth est né le 1 juin 1460, à Yarmouth, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Aldrich, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Mary Tudor, avait 39 ans. Il a épousé Lady Alice Yarmouth en 1472, à Great Yarmouth, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 14 avril 1518, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 57 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Aldrich, Bailiff of Yarmouthが1460年6月1日にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクヤーモウスで生まれた時，父William Aldrichは39歳，母Mary Tudorは39歳であった。1472年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクグレアト ヤーモウスでLady Alice Yarmouthと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1518年4月14日に故郷で57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Aldrich, Bailiff of Yarmouth(이)가 1460년 6월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 야모우스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Aldrich(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Tudor(은)는 39세였다. 그는 1472년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 그리트 야모우스에서 Lady Alice Yarmouth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1518년 4월 14일 그의 고향에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Aldrich, Bailiff of Yarmouth родился 1 июня 1460 г. в местности ярмоут, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William Aldrich было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Tudor, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Alice Yarmouth в 1472 г. в местности грит ярмоут, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 14 апреля 1518 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 57 лет.
